title: Lam Research Faces China Curbs While IBM Pact Reshapes Growth Outlook
detail:
Lam Research Faces China Curbs While IBM Pact Reshapes Growth Outlook
Simply Wall St
Wed, April 29, 2026 at 7:09 PM MST 5 min read
LRCX
IBM
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Find your next quality investment with Simply Wall St's easy and powerful screener, trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

US regulators tighten export controls on Lam Research, restricting shipments of chipmaking tools to China's Hua Hong.

Lam Research is required to halt certain sales to Hua Hong, China's second-largest chipmaker, introducing new regulatory and revenue risks.

Separately, Lam Research and IBM enter a five-year agreement to co-develop advanced semiconductor manufacturing technologies, including High-NA EUV.

Lam Research (NasdaqGS: LRCX) sits at the center of two very different forces right now: heavier US export controls and a new long-term technology partnership. The stock trades at $248.75 after a powerful run, with the share price up 34.4% year to date and 399.9% over three years. That kind of move means fresh news on regulation or technology direction can matter a lot for existing and potential shareholders.

The tighter US rules around shipments to Hua Hong introduce fresh uncertainty around a meaningful slice of Lam's China-related business. At the same time, the five-year IBM collaboration on next-generation fabrication, including High-NA EUV, could help shape Lam's role in future chip manufacturing. Together, these developments reset the mix of risks and opportunities that investors need to weigh when looking at NasdaqGS: LRCX.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Lam Research by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Lam Research.

NasdaqGS:LRCX Earnings & Revenue Growth as at Apr 2026

We've flagged 1 risk for Lam Research. See which could impact your investment.

The export restrictions on shipments to Hua Hong highlight how much Lam Research’s business model is exposed to policy decisions around advanced tools, especially relative to peers like Applied Materials and KLA. Any revenue tied to those specific tools or service contracts is now at risk, and investors have less clarity on how quickly those sales can be redirected to other customers or regions. At the same time, the five year IBM collaboration gives Lam a seat at the table for sub 1 nm and High NA EUV process development. This is where leading edge foundries such as TSMC and Intel are focused. That sort of joint work can influence future tool specifications and process of record wins, which matter for Lam’s long term order book. With recent guidance pointing to expected revenue of US$6.60b plus or minus US$400m and an operating margin of 36.5% plus or minus 1% for the June 2026 quarter, this policy shock and R&D partnership arrive while Lam is already managing a large earnings base and active AI related demand.

title: Is It Time To Revisit IBM (IBM) After The Recent Share Price Pullback?
detail:
Is It Time To Revisit IBM (IBM) After The Recent Share Price Pullback?
Simply Wall St
Wed, April 29, 2026 at 11:07 AM MST 6 min read
IBM
Trade IBM on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Find your next quality investment with Simply Wall St's easy and powerful screener, trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

Investors may be wondering whether International Business Machines at around US$233 a share still offers value, or if the easier gains have already been realized.

The stock is down 8.9% over the past week and 20.1% year to date, while the 3 year and 5 year returns of 106.4% and 104.3% present a very different picture.

Recent headlines have focused on International Business Machines as a key name in large cap tech, with investors weighing its role in software and services against shifting sentiment toward the sector. That context helps explain why short term price moves can differ significantly from the multi year return profile.

Simply Wall St currently assigns International Business Machines a value score of 5 out of 6, based on a set of valuation checks. The rest of this article will walk through those methods before ending with a broader way to think about what the market is really pricing in.

Find out why International Business Machines's -0.2% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Approach 1: International Business Machines Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes projected future cash flows and discounts them back to today, aiming to estimate what those future dollars are worth right now. It is essentially a way of asking what a business is worth based on the cash it is expected to generate for shareholders.

For International Business Machines, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $12.2b. Analyst and extrapolated projections supplied to Simply Wall St run through to 2035, with free cash flow for 2030 estimated at $21.9b. Earlier years, such as 2026, are projected at $15.7b, with later years based on Simply Wall St extrapolations once analyst coverage tapers off.

Discounting these projected cash flows back to today produces an estimated intrinsic value of about $310.24 per share. Compared with a share price around $233, this implies an intrinsic discount of 24.9%, suggesting the stock screens as undervalued under this model.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests International Business Machines is undervalued by 24.9%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

IBM Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026

title: STX Q3 Earnings Top Estimates, Revenues Up on AI-Led Storage Demand
detail:
STX Q3 Earnings Top Estimates, Revenues Up on AI-Led Storage Demand
Zacks Equity Research
Wed, April 29, 2026 at 7:13 AM MST 7 min read
STX
SAP
IBM
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Seagate Technology Holdings plc STX reported third-quarter fiscal 2026 non-GAAP earnings of $4.10 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $3.50 and exceeding the high end of management’s guidance of $3.40 (+/- 20 cents).

The bottom line expanded 115% year over year and 32% sequentially on the back of STX’s strong execution of its strategic objectives and effective use of the technology roadmap to support growing demand.

Non-GAAP revenues of $3.11 billion exceeded the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 5.7%. Revenues also surpassed the high end of guidance, increasing 44% year over year.

STX is operating in a very strong demand environment, especially in data center markets. Management noted that the shift toward inference-driven workloads, agentic AI and multimodal applications is leading to exponential growth in data creation and storage needs.

Seagate Technology Holdings PLC Price, Consensus and EPS Surprise
Seagate Technology Holdings PLC Price, Consensus and EPS Surprise

Seagate Technology Holdings PLC price-consensus-eps-surprise-chart | Seagate Technology Holdings PLC Quote

The March quarter witnessed steady growth in high-capacity nearline drive demand across global cloud and hyperscaler customers. Nearline products accounted for roughly 90% of total exabyte shipments, with capacity largely allocated through calendar 2027.

Modern data centers increasingly need solutions that balance performance with cost efficiency, a trend that strongly favors Seagate’s roadmap. The company’s areal-density-driven strategy aligns well with the long-term growth of AI-generated data, suggesting sustained demand beyond short-term cycles.

The company’s HAMR (Heat-Assisted Magnetic Recording) technology and Mozaic platform remain central to its long-term growth strategy. It began revenue shipments of Mozaic 4 in late March, which can deliver up to 44 terabytes per drive, representing more than 30% higher capacity compared with the first-generation drives. Seagate noted that Mozaic 4+ is projected to constitute the majority of its HAMR exabyte shipments exiting calendar 2026. With the development of Mozaic 5 underway, STX targets to commence qualification shipments of the same in late calendar 2027.

Zacks Investment Research


Image Source: Zacks Investment Research

Following a robust business performance, STX’s shares are up 15.3% in the pre-market trading today. In the past year, shares have gained 536.1% compared with the Zacks Computer-Integrated Systems industry’s rise of 158.1%.

STX's Revenues by End Market

Beginning first-quarter fiscal 2026, STX reports revenues across two key markets — Data Center, encompassing nearline products and systems sold to cloud, enterprise and VIA customers, and Edge IoT, covering consumer and client-focused segments, including network-attached storage.

title: This IBM Stock Bull Spread Allows You to Take Advantage of the Market’s Fear
detail:
This IBM Stock Bull Spread Allows You to Take Advantage of the Market’s Fear
Josh Enomoto
Wed, April 29, 2026 at 6:56 AM MST 5 min read
IBM
Trade IBM on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

It wasn’t the result that speculators were likely anticipating. Although International Business Machines (IBM) delivered the goods for the first quarter, investors rushed for the exits. In terms of raw numbers, the subsequent selloff in IBM stock appears strange. The legacy tech giant posted 9.5% year-over-year growth to $15.92 billion while delivering adjusted earnings per share of $1.91, beating out analysts’ consensus targets.

However, shareholders were looking for a bit more juice, particularly from the company’s Software segment. In addition, management decided to maintain full-year guidance, likely in a bid to be prudent amid uncertainties related to the Iran war. Subsequently, IBM stock responded negatively to the somewhat pensive attitude, with shares down almost 9% in the trailing five sessions.

More News from Barchart

AMD Earnings Bull Put Spread has a High Probability of Success

Massively Disappointing Boston Scientific (BSX) Stock Could Be Due for a Comeback

Unusual Call Option Activity in CVS Ahead of Its Earnings Shows Investors are Bullish

Markets move fast. Keep up by reading our FREE midday Barchart Brief newsletter for exclusive charts, analysis, and headlines.

It’s worth pointing out that the smart money isn’t just treating the soft areas of the financial disclosure as a temporary hiccup. If you look at the volatility skew for the June 18 options chain, you’ll note that implied volatility (IV) scales up significantly as the strike price drops below Tuesday’s closing price of $233.04.

In a purely deductive system, a 10% move up and a 10% move down should have the same IV. However, the fact that the downside right now is more expensive means that the market has a structural bias toward fearing a crash more than it anticipates a melt-up.

In other words, at this moment, professional traders are paying more for accident insurance. However, we have to be clear that this doesn’t necessarily mean an accident is more likely. Instead, we’re observing a shift in cost rather than causality.

To put it simply, if you are bearish (on the debit side) on IBM stock, you would be paying a higher premium than you ordinarily would. However, if you are bullish, you would basically be buying a volatility discount. Specifically, by buying units of volatility at a discount compared to what a bearish trader is paying for the exact same distance move to the downside, you’re arbitraging the market’s fear.

Using the Inductive Method to ‘Justify’ the IBM Stock Trade

While volatility skew is informative, it functionally operates outside the ontology of probabilistic systems. Again, the fact that traders are currently paying more for accident insurance doesn’t necessarily correlate with a higher or lower probability of that accident occurring. It’s just that if the accident does occur and you’re not protected, the cost is likely to be steeper than normal.

title: Omnicom Group Inc. Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary
detail:
Omnicom Group Inc. Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary
Moby Intelligence
Wed, April 29, 2026 at 5:30 AM MST 3 min read
OMC
IBM
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure
Omnicom Group Inc. Q1 2026 Earnings Call Summary - Moby
Strategic Execution and Portfolio Optimization

Management is aggressively realigning the portfolio following the Interpublic acquisition, identifying approximately $3.2 billion in annual revenue for disposal to focus on high-growth 'core operations'.

The integration strategy involved sunsetting or merging over 20 major agency brands in Q1 to consolidate talent and simplify the client interface.

Performance was driven by strong momentum in Integrated Media, which now represents 52% of core revenue, benefiting from a shift toward single-provider marketing solutions.

The Omni platform has been scaled across the entire organization, utilizing Agentic AI to automate manual workflows and improve media ROI through deeper data integration with Acxiom.

Management attributes margin expansion to rapid cost-reduction synergies and the strategic exit from lower-margin, non-core business units.

New business wins with major global brands like IBM and GSK validate the integrated operating model and the combined group's enhanced scale.

Growth Outlook and Synergy Targets

Management reaffirmed its 2026 cost-reduction synergy target of $900 million, with a long-term goal of $1.5 billion by mid-2028.

The company expects to complete a $5 billion share repurchase program by April 2027, projecting an 11% to 12% reduction in share count by year-end 2026.

Guidance assumes a 1% revenue benefit from positive foreign exchange rates for the full year 2026.

The strategic roadmap includes migrating all teams to shared workflow systems and hub locations to further drive operational efficiencies.

Management maintains a 4% constant currency organic growth target for core operations despite broader geopolitical uncertainties.

Integration Costs and Portfolio Dispositions

Recorded $59 million in acquisition integration costs and $34 million in losses related to business dispositions during the first quarter.

Amortization expense increased by $96 million year-over-year, primarily due to intangible assets acquired from Interpublic.

Net interest expense rose by $43 million, reflecting the assumption of $3 billion in debt and new financing activity to support the acquisition.

The Middle East conflict represents a known headwind, though management noted the region accounts for less than 2.5% of total revenue.

Q&A Session Insights
EPS growth trajectory and share count impact

Management expects adjusted EPS growth to accelerate in subsequent quarters, likely exceeding the 11.8% growth seen in Q1.

The share count reduction of 8% to 9% on a weighted average basis will be a significant tailwind for full-year EPS performance.

title: Why IBM Stock Is Climbing Today
detail:
Why IBM Stock Is Climbing Today
Nauman Khan
Tue, April 28, 2026 at 11:08 AM MST 1 min read
IBM
Trade IBM on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

IBM (NYSE:IBM) shares rose about 2% Tuesday after the company said IBM Bob, its enterprise AI development platform, became broadly available on Monday.

Investors appeared to respond to IBM's push deeper into AI software globally.

IBM said Bob spans planning, coding, testing, deployment and modernization, and includes governance and security controls. The system uses multi-model orchestration to route work to models including Anthropic Claude, Mistral open-source tools and IBM Granite.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 5 Warning Signs with NVDA.

Is IBM fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

IBM said more than 80,000 employees now use the platform, up from 100 developers when it launched internally in June 2025. Surveyed users reported a 45% productivity gain, while the Instana team cited a 70% cut in time spent on selected tasks.

IBM said Ernst & Young is using Bob to modernize a global tax platform, and Blue Pearl completed work in three days that normally took weeks. IBM also said its older code-assistant customers will keep support and can move to Bob, which is offered as software as a service with individual and enterprise plans, plus a 30-day trial.
